articles.title: Cotton Corrects Lower on Thursday
articles.expand: Cotton futures saw weakness on Thursday with contracts down 175 to 287 points in the front months. The US dollar index was $0.352 higher at $98.775. Crude oil was up $1 at $102.02. A meeting between President Trump and China’s President Xi in Beijing concluded early this morning. Few details...

articles.title: Fed's Barr warns shrinking balance sheet via liquidity cuts risks stability
articles.expand: Fed Governor Barr said lowering liquidity rules to shrink the Fed's balance sheet is misguided and would increase financial stability risks, arguing the current policy framework has served markets well. articles.title: Pound Sterling breaks down as Labour turmoil overshadows stronger UK GDP data
articles.expand: GBP/USD fell 0.9% on Thursday, breaking below 1.3500 in a sharp staircase decline from session highs to a low close to 1.3395. The move extended a multi-week downtrend from the early-March peak, with bearish momentum building through the European afternoon. The daily candle closed near session lows.

articles.title: More from Fed's Williams, sees no case for rate move as policy sits in good place
articles.expand: Fed's Williams said monetary policy is in a good place and mildly restrictive, seeing no reason to raise or lower rates now, while flagging strong productivity growth and stable market conditions. articles.title: Fed Williams: Job market stabilizes as inflation uncertainty looms
articles.expand: John Williams, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) of New York, spoke on Thursday and said that the job market has stabilized, acknowledging that he is not surprised to see near-term inflation expectations rise.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar slips to fresh session lows ahead of PMI and PPI releases
articles.expand: NZD/USD slipped 0.4% on Thursday, drifting lower through the session to close at fresh daily lows. The pair has been confined to a broad range in recent weeks, unable to retest the early-March peak near 0.6120.

articles.title: Warsh Fed appointment unlikely to deliver rate cuts, analysts warn
articles.expand: Analysts say Kevin Warsh's appointment as Fed chair is unlikely to deliver the rate cuts markets had hoped for, with inflation elevated and the FOMC increasingly hawkish. articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY) rises toward the 98.80 region, reaching fresh two-week highs after US Retail Sales rose 0.5% in April, highlighting resilient consumer spending despite elevated borrowing costs.

articles.expand: TD Securities economists Oscar Munoz and team revise their Fed outlook, now projecting no rate cuts in 2026 as persistent inflation pressures from the Iran conflict, elevated Oil and strained supply chains delay disinflation.

articles.expand: WELLINGTON, May 14 (Bernama-Xinhua) -- Air New Zealand on Thursday warned of a full-year pre-tax loss of 340 to 390 million NZ dollars (US$202 to US$231 million), as surging jet fuel prices driven by West Asia tensions hit earnings, Xinhua reported.
